commands.review_summary:
The PR updates `src/simulate.ts` so the simulator sends `session.type: "realtime"`, requests audio-only Realtime responses, and prints audio transcript events in the terminal.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main sends simulator Realtime payloads that omit `session.type` and request mixed audio/text output, matching the reported API errors. I did not run the live Realtime repro myself in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: No ClawSweeper repair lane is needed because the PR has no actionable patch finding after proof was added.

target.title: fix(simulate): include session.type in session.update payload
target.body:
## Summary
- `vox simulate` currently errors on every input with `vox simulate: session not ready yet` because the OpenAI Realtime API rejects the `session.update` payload sent from `src/simulate.ts`.
- Server response in the call log:
  ```
  invalid_request_error: Missing required parameter: 'session.type'
  ```
- `src/server.ts` already sets `type: "realtime"` via `createRealtimeSessionConfig`, but `simulate.ts` inlines its own session config and was missing it, so `session.updated` never fires and `sessionReady` stays `false`.

## Fix
One-line addition of `type: "realtime"` to the inlined `session.update` payload in `runSimulate`, matching what `server.ts` already does for the Twilio path.

## Test plan
- [x] `npm run typecheck` passes
- [x] `npm run dev -- simulate` reaches `session.updated`, the initial greeting plays, and typed input gets a model response
- [ ] No change to the Twilio path (`server.ts` was already correct)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
target.author: tallmaro
